demos.prompt: Create a demo-ready Three.js visual: concentric audio-reactive geometry, glow, camera motion, browser-safe audio controls, compact code/artifact direction, validation notes, and fallback. Keep it under 90 code lines or 450 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a Three.js creative-coding answer path and the generated Three.js artifact evidence.
demos.fallback_path: Use the generated Three.js artifact and browser full-runtime QA result if live generation or provider access fails.
demos.success_criteria: Real Three.js package renders the artifact nonblank in browser QA; no unsupported DCC/MCP or autonomous execution claims.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: Three.js browser rendering passed through a temporary QA runtime; the app dependency graph was not changed.

demos.prompt: Create a demo-ready p5.js generative morphogenesis sketch using reaction diffusion, flow fields, particles, branching, and emergent form. Include controls, browser-safe notes, source boundaries, compact artifact direction, and fallback. Keep it under 90 code lines or 450 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a p5.js generative visual with practical morphogenesis technique guidance and a clear visual growth story.
demos.fallback_path: Use the p5 golden artifact and browser full-runtime QA result if live generation fails.
demos.success_criteria: Real p5 package renders the artifact nonblank in browser QA; no metaphysical, medical, religious, or historical authority claim.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: The p5 artifact now uses real p5 runtime validation, not shim-only evidence.

demos.prompt: Design a demo-ready GLSL fragment shader or post-processing visual. Include uniforms, resolution/time handling, glow, texture movement, WebGL risks, compact artifact direction, and static fallback. Keep it under 80 shader lines or 400 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows shader/post-processing guidance and the generated GLSL artifact.
demos.fallback_path: Use glsl_kaleidoscope_field.frag and WebGL QA evidence if live preview is unavailable.
demos.success_criteria: GLSL compiles, links, draws, and pixel-checks nonblank in local WebGL.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: GLSL compiles directly in WebGL; display-FPS benchmarking is not claimed.

demos.prompt: Create a bounded Hydra feedback-pattern demo for the validated local hydra-synth browser artifact path. Use oscillator layers, modulation, feedback, output routing, runtime assumptions, and GLSL/static fallback. Keep it under 60 Hydra lines or 350 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a bounded Hydra-compatible pattern chain and the exact runtime support boundary.
demos.fallback_path: Use hydra_feedback_lattice.js and browser full-runtime QA result if live generation or preview fails.
demos.success_criteria: Hydra synth artifact renders nonblank through real local hydra-synth QA with no microphone or full editor claim.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: Hydra is bounded to a validated local hydra-synth browser artifact path.

demos.prompt: Answer a creative-coding runtime question with registered source grounding. In under 350 words, name the retrieved sources, source boundaries, browser validation steps, and retrieval fallback.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows retrieval-grounded guidance and explains source boundaries.
demos.fallback_path: Use redacted latest-live RAGAs, sanitized RAGAs result rows, and retrieval smoke evidence if live retrieval is unavailable.
demos.success_criteria: Local Chroma retrieval returns results for 7/7 capstone scenarios; redacted latest-live RAGAs has 4 rows, 0 skipped, 0 metric failures across context_precision, faithfulness, and answer_relevancy.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: Raw private live-session text stays local, while the redacted latest-live fixture now has provider-backed RAGAs metrics.

demos.prompt: Translate threshold, recursion, and return into a practical browser visual system. Cover geometry, motion, color, runtime, interaction, constraints, and claim boundaries. Avoid spiritual, therapeutic, or authority claims. Keep it under 400 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows abstract concept language translated into operational visual structure.
demos.fallback_path: Use the p5 artifact, prompt library, and claim-safety explanation.
demos.success_criteria: Concept language remains aesthetic/operational; p5 artifact renders nonblank through real p5 QA.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: The demo converts concept language into browser implementation guidance without authority claims.

demos.prompt: Design a geometry/morphogenesis browser visual system using radial structure, recursive growth, reaction diffusion, branching, flow fields, and particles. Include runtime choice, preview strategy, source boundaries, compact artifact direction, and fallback. Keep it under 450 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows generative system planning with geometry, computational growth, and practical runtime choices.
demos.fallback_path: Use the p5.js and GLSL golden QA records plus offline prompts if live generation or preview fails.
demos.success_criteria: Output remains implementation guidance with source boundaries and browser-runtime constraints.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: This flow demonstrates rule-based visual systems and emergent form without unsupported authority claims.

demos.prompt: Plan a browser-based installation or immersive scene for a gallery demo. Include concept, geometry, audience movement, runtimes, retrieval needs, preview plan, artifact package, evaluation checks, fallback, and handoff boundaries. Keep it under 450 words.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a project-bundle planning handoff with explicit local-demo and future-scope boundaries.
demos.fallback_path: Use the integrated Demo Mode list, offline dataset, launcher fallback, and evidence boundaries; do not claim live DCC/MCP, autonomous delivery, or public deployment.
demos.success_criteria: Planner output is bounded as project planning and handoff notes only.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: This is local browser installation planning, not cloud deployment or external tool execution.
